task_completion.description: Open the SLP climatology dataset in Panoply.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open, showing 'slp.mon.ltm.nc' in the dataset browser or sources panel.

task_completion.description: Generate a Northern Hemisphere SLP map for January.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a map of Sea Level Pressure (SLP). The plot's time setting should indicate January (e.g., 'Jan' or time index 1), and the map projection or focus should be on the Northern Hemisphere.
A Panoply save dialog, file manager, or terminal output showing the file 'nh_jan_slp_map.png' being saved to or existing in the '~/Documents/AviationSafety/' directory.

task_completion.description: Identify the deepest low-pressure system in the Northern Hemisphere for January.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing 'altimetry_hazard_report.txt' with text identifying the deepest low (e.g., Icelandic Low) and its minimum pressure (approximately 993.88 or 994 hPa).

task_completion.description: Calculate and document the maximum theoretical terrain clearance error.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'altimetry_hazard_report.txt' file showing the calculated terrain clearance error of approximately 570 to 574 feet (derived from [1013 - 994] * 30).

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to visualize the dataset rather than bypassing it.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Panoply graphical interface being interacted with (e.g., creating plots, adjusting map projections, exporting images).

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or CLI tool to bypass the Panoply map generation requirement.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows running Python scripts (e.g., using matplotlib or netCDF4 libraries) or CDO/NCO commands to generate the map image.

integrity.description: The agent manually created and wrote the hazard report.
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, gedit) or echo/cat commands in the terminal showing the agent actively typing or formatting the contents of 'altimetry_hazard_report.txt'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The standard aviation sea-level pressure is 1013 hPa, and the altitude error rule of thumb is 30 ft/hPa. The deepest low-pressure system in the Northern Hemisphere in January is the Icelandic Low, with an actual minimum SLP of approximately 993.88 hPa (accept ~994 hPa). The theoretical terrain clearance error should be calculated as (1013 - 993.88) * 30, which equals approximately 573.6 feet (accept values in the range of 570-574 feet).
